# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8090; // or get it dynamically if you want to run on Deno Deploy edge runtime, but this is not recommended due to security issues 
import { readConfig } from "./config_reader.ts" ;  
// assuming the config reader module reads environment variables and returns a configuration object with paths of modules user wants loaded in their application (user can specify custom ones)
const allowedModules = ["module1", "module2"]; // list all available plugins here 
import { loadModule } from './loader.ts';  
// assuming the loader module dynamically loads and returns a promise that resolves to an instance of loaded plugin or null if it fails (e.g., not found)
const app = async () => {
    const request = await Deno.request(Deno.env.get("REQUEST_URL") || "http://localhost:" + port);  // get the module path from environment variables, otherwise default to local server if running on localhost or a specific url set by user in env variable REQUEST_URL (set it using deno deploy)
    const config = await readConfig();  
     // assuming we have an instance of our configuration object that contains paths for modules the app is interested 
      let modulePaths: string[] | undefined;
       if(config.moduleList){
         modulePaths= [...new Set([ ...config.moduleList, '.'].concat(allowedModules))]; // ensure we have all allowed plugins and a default one at the end ('.') 
      } else {  
        modulePaths = allowedModules;   
       };    
         for await (const path of modulePaths) {// load each plugin in turn, if it fails then move on to next. If no modules are left return from function and exit the app gracefully 
           try{
             const mod=await Deno.run(`--allow-read --allow-write ${{path}}:$port/mods_dir `);   // run a new process with read & write access to plugin directory, this is necessary for dynamic import() and will not be allowed by edge computing security
             const modInstance = await loadModule({ path });  // dynamically loads the module specified in request url (user can specify custom ones) using Deno.run(). If it fails then move on next iteration of loop as we have already loaded all plugins, if successful add instance to list and continue with rest iterations
             console.log(`Loaded ${path} successfully! `);  // log the path that was just read from request url (user specified module) in logs  
           } catch {   
               Deno.delete(mod),// delete any existing mod process if it fails to load, this is necessary for cleanup after failed attempts at loading plugins and not leave dangling processes around 
             }; // end of try/catch block    
         },     
          console.log("All modules loaded successfully!");  
        }    else {// If no more module paths left then exit the app gracefully, this is necessary to prevent edge computing security and ensure all resources are cleaned up properly 
           Deno.exit(); // or throw new Error('No allowed plugins found!') if you want a different behavior in case none of them were loaded successfully  
        }    };// end async function     app;      });       serve(async (req) => { return await app() }, `http://localhost:${port}`);  // start the server. This is necessary for Deno Deploy, but not recommended in a local environment as it can expose your application to potential security risks!
